desc: Siquijor is a compact Philippine island of waterfalls and reef coves where access is usually multi-leg (international/domestic flight plus ferry), best for relaxed travelers comfortable with flexible timings and scooter-based exploration.
hls: Signature: Cambugahay Falls and Tubod Marine Sanctuary in one compact island loop
Under-the-radar: inland Bandilaan viewpoints and quiet village cafés away from sunset strips
Less developed vibe than major Visayas resort islands
Best for 3-5 night slow stays rather than checklist hopping

todo.name: Cambugahay Falls at opening time
todo.desc: Signature Siquijor stop—go early for clearer pools and fewer queues at rope-swing spots.

todo.name: Tubod Marine Sanctuary snorkel
todo.desc: Signature reef session with a local guide to time currents and visibility.

todo.name: Lazi church and convent
todo.desc: Visit one of the oldest stone church complexes in the Visayas.

todo.name: Island scooter loop via Enrique Villanueva coast
todo.desc: Under-the-radar coastal stretches are quieter than the west-side sunset strip.

todo.name: Mt. Bandilaan forest reserve
todo.desc: Under-the-radar inland hike for cooler air and panoramic island viewpoints.

todo.name: Paliton Beach sunset
todo.desc: Finish with a classic west-coast golden-hour swim and shoreline bars.

wifi.note: Improving in beachfront stays and café clusters, but outages still occur during storms and more remote inland properties can be less consistent.

prac.fltNote: UK/Ireland routes typically connect via Manila, Cebu, Singapore, or Doha, then combine domestic flight and ferry; Amihan dry season (Dec-May) brings stronger demand and pricing.
prac.currency: Philippine peso (PHP); cash is still useful for scooter fuel, tricycle rides, small eateries, and some marine-fee or ferry-related payments despite growing card acceptance in better resorts.
prac.lang: Filipino and English are official; Cebuano/Bisaya is widely spoken in Siquijor.
prac.tz: PHT (UTC+8) year-round; no daylight saving time.
prac.visa: The Philippines allows many nationalities visa-free short stays, but onward-ticket and passport-validity checks are commonly enforced at airline check-in and arrival, so confirm the latest rule for your passport before booking flexible ferry combinations.
alerts: Ferry crossings from Dumaguete/Tagbilaran can be delayed or canceled in monsoon surges and typhoon-adjacent weather.
Power and mobile-data interruptions still happen during heavy rain; keep offline tickets/maps and a cash buffer.
Narrow island roads become slick after downpours, and night riding has limited lighting outside town centers.

sweet: Late Feb-Jun for the best blend of lower rain, longer daylight, and more reliable ferries; Jul-Aug is a workable shoulder if you accept wetter crossings.
